instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
InsurTech: Impact of block chain and Bitcoin on life insurance
Block chain technology has been pegged as the game changer within the finance world. Just like every other aspect being disrupted by technology, Insurance is likely to see some revolutionary change in the coming decade. Block chain technology has been a dominant topic trending since 2017. It is likely to be in focus over the next few years. The insurance sector has not been open to transitioning from legacy systems until now. However, things are likely to change in the future. The advent of DeFi, specifically Bitcoin and NFT(Non-fungible tokens), is pegged to change the face of insurance evaluation. At present, RBI does not recognise or validate the existence of cryptocurrency. However, we do not know what the future holds for us!  Here we look at the impact of block chain and bitcoin on insurance in a detailed manner.
Disrupters in the form of Bitcoin and NFTs:
With the advent of Bitcoin and NFTs, the valuation has become extremely complex. They do not conform to the conventional valuation methodology, and their accounting practices are not standardised, making it tough to evaluate and ascertain the value of these instruments. It thus doesn’t give a clear picture of the actual net worth of an individual. This problem persists with corporates as well. These assets are valued as intangible assets. However, they are highly speculative in nature and do not necessarily conform with conventional intangible assets.
While DeFi continues to remain evasive, the underlying technology (blockchain) has garnered immense attention for its galore benefits.
Understanding block chain technology:
Within the block chain Framework, each block represents a record of a new transaction. It could represent a cryptocurrency's location, patient medical data or voting records of an individual. Each block, after completion, will be clubbed alongside other blocks leading to the creation of a chain of blocks, thus the name block chain.
Block chain enables people to write records of Information and control the mode of updating these records. Each block is provided with an encrypted Unique Identification which is never revealed during the entire transaction process. This enhances the security and transparency of the transaction. The privacy of the individuals conducting the transaction is also maintained throughout the process.
Insurance backed by block chain technology
In the insurance world, block chain technology can help optimise risk profiling of the individual or organisation insured. By using block chain technology, shared infrastructure can be achieved, wherein the medical assessments of the applicant can be maintained in a centralised manner accessible across various insurers. This ensures that repetitive medical tests, documentation, and evaluation are not required. Implementation of block chain technology in life insurance can lead to the enhanced operational efficiency of insurance companies.
The key benefits of block chain technology in insurance are mentioned below:
Benefits for life insurers
Efficient fraud detection:
Life insurance in the industry is plagued by fake claims. About 90% of the life insurance frauds fall within the bracket of Rs. 1 lakh to Rs. 10 lakh. The decentralised public ledger provides enhanced security in the form of encryption to detect any fraudulent activities. The insurers will also be equipped to identify duplicate transactions and prevent any thereof. Multiple currency transactions can be streamlined, and discrepancies associated with such transactions can be eliminated.
Reduced cost:
One of the key benefits that life insurance companies can derive from implementing block chain is the reduction in administrative costs. Life insurers can reduce costs by automated verification of applicants’ identities, evaluation of contract validity, and automated audit of claims and data from third parties.
Enhanced transparency:
The key challenge for insurance companies is the lack of transparency and delays around the claims process and settlement. This has alienated customers. The block chain framework can rebuild trust by enhancing the robustness of claims processes and streamlining claims management effectively.
Improvement in data quality:
Implementation of block chain technology is likely to benefit speciality insurance and reinsurance. Block chain can improve the data quality for both front-end and back-end office sources. It can eliminate errors and inefficiencies; bridge the gap that exists between end-users and insurers.
Product innovation:
Block chain Technology can facilitate using alternative data to arrive at accurate actuarial models. Analytics can help us identify high-risk items and customise the offerings, identify a new user base and device products which are aligned with their requirement.
Robust issuance of policies: We already see a sea of changes in how insurance is drawn and risk is assessed. From physical applications, we are now at issuance of online term plans. The robustness will only enhance rapidly with the implementation of blockchain technology.
Benefits for life insurance policyholders:
Better claim experience:With blockchain, the claim process can be simplified, the audit of the claim is automated, and after the verification of the claim, it is recorded within the framework to trigger contract execution. The process will be streamlined and automated in such a manner that the time taken is much lower than the current process. The reduced waiting time for the beneficiary works in favour of the life insurance company as its brand value increases with timely relief provided to the distressed family.
Instant payouts: The fund disbursement will be guided by seamless interactions between insurers and policyholders. The interactions will be more direct, and many of the processes can be automated. Given the digital advancements, the authenticity of the claim can be established, the identity of the claimant can be verified, and payment can be transferred to the policyholder/claimant’s digital wallets seamlessly.
Limitations of blockchain technology:
With advanced technology comes the fear of the unknown, and there is the possibility of cyber-attacks which could be quite damaging as a huge quantum of customer data could be compromised. If the entry of data is not done with accuracy, it defeats the purpose of the implementation of the entire framework. Downtime or software crashes could be detrimental to the business and operational efficiency. The complexity of the framework also adds to the limitations of adapting quickly to the changing ecosystem of the business.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article is not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Ltd., Regd. office Address: Bajaj Allianz House, Airport Road, Yerawada, Pune - 411006, Reg. No.: 116, CIN: U66010PN2001PLC015959, call us on toll free No.: 1800 209 7272, Mail us: customercare@bajajallianz.co.in, Fax No: 02066026789
